free_text: Measurement of neutron activation cross sections
in the energy range between 2. and 7. MeV by using
a Ti-deuteron Target and a deuteron gas target.
Ref. R,JAERI-M-90-171,1990, first author K.Kawade, is
given as ref. for experiment in FNS, but does not
contain details for energy range 2-7 MeV.
FNS facility description in report of T.Nakamura,Proc.
4th Symposium on Accelerator Science and Technology,
Data was clarified by authors as preliminary,
but a publication of final data was not found.
3-4.5 MeV, Ti board of 0.5mm thick, water-cooled,
d+ beam of 10 microA and 0.35-1.4 MeV; typical neutron
flux in deuteron energy of 1.4 MeV - 10**6 n/cm**2/sec
at 1 cm from the surface of Ti board.
Sample was sandwiched by two indium foils of
Irradiated at 0 deg. to incident deuteron beam and at
1.0 cm from the surface of Ti-board. Pneumatic
transport system was used to move samples from
NE213 scintillator to monitor fluctuation of
neutron flux during irradiation at interval of 10 sec.
Coaxial type 22% and well-type HPGe detectors to
Efficiencies in the bottom of well-type detector were
6-7 times larger than those at surface position of

than calculated value. due to deuteron beam size and
